description:
You are helping a teammate who is writing a few one-off shell utilities for log analysis.  
A tiny sample syslog file has already been placed at  

  /home/user/logs/syslog_sample.log  

Your assignment is to build two artefacts, entirely by using ordinary shell commands (no compiled code, no Python/Ruby/Perl, etc.):

1. /home/user/logs/filtered_syslog.log  
   • Must contain every line from syslog_sample.log whose severity token is exactly the word “WARN” or exactly the word “ERROR”.  
   • Matching is case-sensitive and must rely on a regular-expression (egrep/grep ‑E, sed, awk… are all acceptable).  
   • The order of the lines must not change, and the text of every kept line must be byte-for-byte identical to the original line (including any spaces).  
   • No header, footer or blank lines may be added—just the filtered lines themselves.

2. /home/user/logs/summary.log  
   This file is a three-line tally that has to be produced exclusively from the contents of filtered_syslog.log.  
   The exact format that the automated test will check is:

     WARN: <number_of_warn_lines>
     ERROR: <number_of_error_lines>
     ERROR_CODES: <comma-separated_list_of_distinct_error_codes_in_original_order>

   • “WARN:” and “ERROR:” must be upper-case and followed by a single space, then the integer count, then a single Unix newline (LF).  
   • “ERROR_CODES:” must be upper-case and followed by a single space, then the distinct values that appear inside “[code=…]” fields on the ERROR lines, in the same order in which they first occurred in the original log. Separate the codes with a literal comma and NO spaces. Finish the line with exactly one newline.  
   • If, for some reason, no ERROR lines exist, the value after “ERROR_CODES:” must be the literal string “NONE”.

You should be able to accomplish everything with roughly three or four shell commands:
  a. a regular-expression filter for WARN/ERROR  
  b. perhaps a second command to pull out the code substrings  
  c. simple counting commands (wc ‑l, awk, etc.)  
  d. echo/printf (or similar) to write the summary.log

Important notes:
• Do not move or rename the original syslog_sample.log file.  
• Do not create any additional files or directories outside /home/user/logs/.  
• Both output files must be world-readable (chmod 644 is fine) so the grading scripts can inspect them.  
• If you need temporary files, remove them when you are done.  
• When you believe everything is correct, an `ls -l /home/user/logs` should show exactly:  
     filtered_syslog.log  
     summary.log  
     syslog_sample.log  
  and nothing else.

The grader will:
  1. Verify that filtered_syslog.log contains the right four lines in the right order.  
  2. Verify that summary.log matches the exact three-line format and correct values.  

No additional output is required from your terminal session—creating the files in the correct state is sufficient.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts):

File: /home/user/logs/syslog_sample.log  
Contents (7 lines, each ends with LF):
2023-08-25 10:02:15 server1 sshd[1234]: INFO User root logged in
2023-08-25 10:03:10 server1 sshd[1234]: WARN Failed password attempt for user dev
2023-08-25 10:05:42 server1 app[5678]: ERROR Could not connect to database [code=DBCONN01]
2023-08-25 10:06:00 server1 kernel: INFO CPU temperature normal
2023-08-25 10:07:18 server1 app[5678]: ERROR Timeout while reading socket [code=SOCKTIME02]
2023-08-25 10:08:22 server1 app[5678]: WARN Deprecated API usage detected
2023-08-25 10:09:30 server1 backup[9012]: INFO Backup completed successfully

The grader expects the following final state:

File: /home/user/logs/filtered_syslog.log  
Exact contents (4 lines):
2023-08-25 10:03:10 server1 sshd[1234]: WARN Failed password attempt for user dev
2023-08-25 10:05:42 server1 app[5678]: ERROR Could not connect to database [code=DBCONN01]
2023-08-25 10:07:18 server1 app[5678]: ERROR Timeout while reading socket [code=SOCKTIME02]
2023-08-25 10:08:22 server1 app[5678]: WARN Deprecated API usage detected

File: /home/user/logs/summary.log  
Exact contents (3 lines, final LF at end of each line):
WARN: 2
ERROR: 2
ERROR_CODES: DBCONN01,SOCKTIME02

Permissions: both filtered_syslog.log and summary.log must have mode 0644 (regular user read/write, group/other read).  
No other files should exist in /home/user/logs/ when the task ends.
